labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose

criticalHigh.action: Review for causes of AKI, correct volume depletion, reassess concomitant medications

labs.actionRequired: Review for causes of AKI, correct volume depletion, reassess concomitant medications if >30% increase
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute kidney injury and dose-dependent effects

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose
labs.actionRequired: Review concurrent drugs, moderate potassium intake, consider diuretics, sodium bicarbonate, or potassium binders if hyperkalemia develops
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for hyperkalemia, especially with concurrent ACEi/ARB use

criticalLow.action: Check for hypovolemia, adjust diuretic dose, discontinue other nephrotoxic agents

labs.actionRequired: Check for hypovolemia, adjust diuretic dose, discontinue other nephrotoxic agents if >30% decline
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for acute decline in kidney function, especially when used with SGLT2i

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR and depends on the type of diuretic used. Use caution in patients with advanced CKD taking potassium-sparing diuretics due to hyperkalemia risk.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Monitor serum creatinine and potassium within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose of ACEi or ARB. Diuretics can precipitate acute kidney injury (AKI) and electrolyte abnormalities, and the hypokalemic response to diuretics is diminished with low eGFR
